claim_text: However, its use in studying brain tumors is somewhat limited by high gray matter FDG uptake14.

sub_claims.text: FDG-PET's utility for studying brain tumors is limited because gray matter takes up FDG at high levels, reducing tumor-to-background contrast.

evidence.snippet: Both high-grade gliomas and gray-matter structures take up 18 F-FDG avidly. Thus, when tumors are in or near gray matter, it may be difficult to distinguish between the two.
This investigation aimed to show that delaying 18 F-FDG PET of gliomas beyond the standard imaging time of approximately 60 min better distinguishes tumor from gray matter.
Delineation of gliomas from both gray matter and white matter improves both visually and by SUV analysis when the interval between 18 F-FDG administration and PET data acquisition is extended.

Based on these observations, we hypothesized that delineation of gliomas with 18 F-FDG PET could be improved by extending the interval between 18 F-FDG administration and PET data acquisition due to slower dephosphorylation of FDG-6-phosphate in tumor relative to gray matter.

sub_claims.nuance: spence2004 directly states that avid gray-matter FDG uptake makes distinguishing tumors from adjacent gray matter difficult, and proposes delayed imaging as a mitigation. The manuscript's limitation claim is well-grounded by this source.

phrasings_tried: FDG uptake in normal gray matter limits brain tumor FDG-PET
gliomas are hard to distinguish from gray matter on FDG-PET because gray matter takes up FDG
delayed imaging improves tumor-to-gray-matter contrast in FDG-PET of gliomas
high gray matter FDG avidity reduces tumor conspicuity
attestation.indirect_attribution_check: spence2004 cites prior work on delayed/dual-time-point FDG PET in gray-matter/tumor differentiation but is itself a primary paper establishing the delayed-interval protocol; the limitation of gray-matter avidity is asserted directly in its introduction.
attestation.out_of_context_check: The manuscript uses spence2004 as evidence of a known FDG-PET limitation; spence2004 frames the same fact as motivation for its methodological contribution (delayed imaging). Usage is consistent.

sibling_verdicts.excerpt: Not ingested in this run (co-cite marker '5,13' on a prior sentence about tumor metabolism measurement; not the marker '14' that cites spence2004).
Not ingested in this run; attached to marker '5,13' on prior sentence, not marker '14'.
